name: Dr. Simon Vale
bio: A celebrated behavioral neurologist whose public charm hid a rigid, controlling management style.
motive: Not applicable.
secrets: Concealed a relationship with Daniel Kwon.
Prepared a police referral after reviewing gala accounts.
statements: Publicly thanked operations staff only as a group during the gala speech.
Told Rachel Ives the institute would be 'cleaner by morning.'
knownTimeline: 8:05 PM gave speech with subtle word errors.
8:49 PM drafted an incomplete email mentioning C, accounts, and corridor.
9:02 PM locked his office from inside.

name: Clara Finch
bio: A precise, understated coordinator who knew every route, badge reader, schedule change, and emergency workaround in the building.
motive: Possible resentment over public credit and pressure from a private family crisis.
secrets: Keeps Martin Finch's care facility situation private.
Has more access to service corridors than most senior staff.
statements: Says Simon seemed irritated but lucid after the corridor incident.
Insists she spent the key window handling caterer timing near the atrium.
knownTimeline: 4:18 PM asked Maryam about Simon's alternate route.
6:18 PM signed out Noah's toolkit.
7:04 PM entered the corridor storage alcove.

name: Evelyn Vale
bio: A practiced public partner who helped build Simon's donor network while privately preparing to leave him.
motive: Divorce negotiations, reputation damage, and anger over Simon's secrecy.
secrets: Had a draft separation agreement.
Knew Simon was having an affair but not with whom.
statements: Says she was on the atrium stage during the central gala sequence.
Claims Simon was still performing well enough to charm donors at 8:05 PM.
knownTimeline: 7:35 PM joined the stage program.
7:42 PM visible on stage beside Felix Ward.
8:05 PM watched Simon deliver his speech.

name: Jonah Vale
bio: A volatile graduate dropout who resented being treated as a fragile extension of Simon's public image.
motive: Family anger, financial dependence, and humiliation.
secrets: Sent Simon a threatening text the week before the gala.
Was trying to sell a family watch to cover debts.
statements: Says the glass elevator jammed with him inside during the key corridor window.
Admits he wanted a confrontation later that night.
knownTimeline: 7:38 PM entered the glass elevator.
7:42 PM visible inside the stalled elevator.
8:22 PM argued with Evelyn near the donor lounge.

name: Dr. Lena Ortiz
bio: A brilliant clinician-administrator passed over whenever Simon wanted full credit for institutional successes.
motive: Career succession and anger over stalled reforms.
secrets: Had drafted a board memo questioning Simon's leadership.
Knew donor confidence was weakening.
statements: Says she was speaking live in the atrium during the true fatal window.
Claims Simon's speech errors were new and medically concerning.
knownTimeline: 7:37 PM began the live neuroethics segment.
7:42 PM on camera before the audience.
8:14 PM handed Simon a donor folder.

name: Dr. Marcus Hale
bio: A severe ethics officer who believed Simon treated compliance as a public relations obstacle.
motive: Professional conflict over suppressed research complaints.
secrets: Kept a private file on Simon's consent practices.
Met with a journalist before the gala.
statements: Says he was logged into the dual-access laboratory with a research fellow.
Believes Simon was capable of staging distractions to avoid scandal.
knownTimeline: 7:30 PM entered the dual-access laboratory.
7:42 PM recorded inside the lab by two-person access logs.
8:28 PM returned to the atrium.

name: Mira Sol
bio: A former patient who turned Simon's own language of memory and agency against him in public protests.
motive: Personal grievance and activist anger.
secrets: Entered the building earlier with a press badge before being escorted out.
Had a printed speech naming Simon.
statements: Says she was outside the south plaza during the corridor window.
Denies touching any exhibit equipment.
knownTimeline: 7:20 PM escorted outside.
7:42 PM filmed by press on the south plaza.
9:45 PM interviewed by local news.

name: Ezra Cole
bio: A wealthy donor who expected naming rights and private access in exchange for his generosity.
motive: Threatened funding dispute and embarrassment over a delayed pledge.
secrets: His foundation had not actually transferred the final pledge.
A message falsely invoked his name.
statements: Says he left the institute before the fatal corridor window.
Denies sending any donor-page message to Simon.
knownTimeline: 7:22 PM departed through the west entrance.
7:39 PM his name appeared in a message he says he did not send.
8:10 PM phone pinged from a private club.

name: Noah Reed
bio: A meticulous fabricator who designed the mobile Memory Arch installation.
motive: Contract pressure and fear of blame if the exhibit failed.
secrets: Had argued with operations about last-minute exhibit movement.
Photographed the arch after inspection to protect himself.
statements: Says he left the Memory Arch safe at 5:55 PM.
Says his toolkit was borrowed after his inspection.
knownTimeline: 5:55 PM completed Memory Arch inspection.
6:18 PM toolkit signed out by operations.
7:42 PM at the loading dock with his crew.

name: Owen Pike
bio: A tired security chief blamed for every camera gap and donor inconvenience.
motive: Risk of losing his job after repeated access failures.
secrets: Disabled a nuisance alarm without logging the reason.
Privately disliked Simon's habit of overruling security.
statements: Says he was in the control room during the corridor incident.
Confirms Clara's lanyard had access to the storage alcove.
knownTimeline: 7:32 PM returned to control room.
7:42 PM control room audio logs capture his radio traffic.
10:08 PM opened the locked office under emergency protocol.

name: Daniel Kwon
bio: A gifted junior researcher whose career depended on Simon's patronage and discretion.
motive: Jealousy, career fear, and the risk of exposure.
secrets: Was secretly involved with Simon.
Visited Simon's office after the speech and gave him water.
statements: Says Simon seemed frightened and confused at 8:37 PM.
Denies touching the bronze model.
knownTimeline: 7:42 PM led a demonstration in the East Gallery.
8:37 PM visited Simon's office.
8:41 PM left Simon alone.

name: Maryam Hart
bio: Simon's capable assistant, responsible for calendar changes, donor routing, and urgent message triage.
motive: Exhaustion and resentment after years of impossible demands.
secrets: Forwarded a donor-route change without verifying its source.
Knew Simon was reviewing personnel files.
statements: Says Clara asked about Simon's alternate route earlier.
Admits she redirected Simon to Service Corridor C at 7:40 PM.
knownTimeline: 4:18 PM discussed Simon's alternate route with Clara.
7:40 PM redirected Simon to Service Corridor C.
8:49 PM saw Simon's incomplete email draft alert.

name: Priya Sen
bio: A cautious accountant who noticed duplicated vendor payments tied to gala accessibility invoices.
motive: Concern about being blamed for finance irregularities.
secrets: Delayed escalating the duplicate payments because senior staff were involved.
Kept her own copy of the payment trail.
statements: Says she sent Simon a note at 3:10 PM.
Claims Simon understood the payment trail by 3:24 PM.
knownTimeline: 3:10 PM sent Simon note about duplicate vendor payments.
3:24 PM saw Simon open the connected ledger.
8:55 PM tried to find Maryam.

name: Tessa Wynn
bio: A documentary photographer hired to make the gala look effortless.
motive: None apparent beyond protecting her contract.
secrets: Deleted several blurry frames before realizing one held a useful reflection.
Moved through service paths to avoid blocking donors.
statements: Says the Memory Arch looked intact in her 6:52 PM corridor photo.
Later finds a reflection near Service Corridor C at 7:41 PM.
knownTimeline: 6:52 PM photographed Service Corridor C.
7:41 PM captured a reflective photo near the corridor.
8:05 PM photographed Simon's speech.

name: Ana Brooks
bio: A calm catering lead trying to move service carts through an overcrowded institute.
motive: Wanted to protect her team from blame for a service-route incident.
secrets: Initially repeated a stronger alibi than she personally observed.
Was asked to describe Clara's work as continuous.
statements: Admits Clara asked her to call the alibi continuous.
Says she saw Clara in short intervals, not without gaps.
knownTimeline: 7:00 PM staged desserts near the service junction.
7:12 PM saw Clara return toward the atrium.
8:30 PM supervised cleanup near donor lounge.

name: Sophie Park
bio: An emergency physician attending as a guest of the institute's clinical advisory group.
motive: No known motive.
secrets: Had treated a prior donor incident the institute preferred to keep quiet.
Noticed Simon's symptoms before others took them seriously.
statements: Says she offered Simon medical help at 7:44 PM.
Says Clara minimized the corridor strike and encouraged the program to continue.
knownTimeline: 7:44 PM offered Simon medical help.
8:05 PM watched his speech for neurological signs.
10:08 PM joined emergency response.

name: Eli Grant
bio: A practical maintenance technician who understood how service fixtures were moved and secured.
motive: Frustration after Simon cut overtime and blamed facilities for delays.
secrets: Skipped a low-priority handrail repair to handle gala lights.
Knew which alcove locks were sticking.
statements: Says the Memory Arch should not roll if the protective edge and brake stop are attached.
Confirms the storage alcove reader was active that night.
knownTimeline: 6:45 PM fixed atrium light controls.
7:04 PM received a service ping near Corridor C.
9:30 PM checked office HVAC after a complaint.

name: Keisha Moore
bio: A consultant whose careful accessibility work was often repackaged by institute leadership.
motive: Professional anger over credit and unpaid revision work.
secrets: Privately warned Clara that Simon would never credit operations fairly.
Kept annotated accessibility plans.
statements: Says Clara was proud of the route redesign despite Simon claiming it publicly.
Says Clara knew exactly which corridor route Simon used when avoiding crowds.
knownTimeline: 5:30 PM reviewed accessibility signage.
7:15 PM spoke with Clara near the atrium.
8:05 PM heard Simon take credit for operational improvements.

name: Rachel Ives
bio: A skeptical reporter invited after threatening to publish a piece on the institute's patient controversies.
motive: Professional benefit from scandal, not an obvious physical motive.
secrets: Met Marcus Hale for background before the gala.
Recorded Simon's odd phrasing after the speech.
statements: Says Simon used strange wording at 8:43 PM.
Says he seemed to understand questions but chose the wrong words.
knownTimeline: 7:50 PM questioned board members.
8:43 PM spoke with Simon.
9:20 PM called her editor about a possible medical issue.

name: Felix Ward
bio: A polished board chairman invested in keeping the institute solvent and scandal-free.
motive: Wanted Simon contained, not necessarily dead.
secrets: Knew the board was considering leadership changes.
Pressed Simon to delay bad news until after the gala.
statements: Says Simon was planning a personnel action after the gala.
Confirms Evelyn was on stage beside him during the fatal window.
knownTimeline: 7:35 PM stood on stage with Evelyn.
8:05 PM introduced Simon's speech.
9:10 PM looked for Simon to discuss donor calls.

name: Martin Finch
bio: Clara's brother, a patient at a private care facility outside the institute.
motive: No access to the gala, but his care bills create pressure around Clara.
secrets: His facility warned of termination if payments stopped.
He did not know institute funds were being discussed.
statements: Cannot place any call to Simon on gala night.
Facility staff say he was not at the institute.
knownTimeline: Gala night records place him at the care facility.
Payment warnings were active before the gala.

name: Detective Rowan Price
bio: The local detective assigned after institute security preserved the locked office scene.
motive: No suspect motive.
secrets: Worries the office scene is too neat.
Keeps the full autopsy interpretation restricted until the player connects the evidence.
statements: The case needs a report, not a hunch.
A correct accusation must explain time, place, method, motive, and eliminations.
knownTimeline: 10:08 PM arrived after security called emergency services.
10:26 PM sealed the office.
Next morning requested a deeper scene reconstruction.

name: Dr. Amara Bell
bio: A direct forensic physician who cares more about injury mechanics than dramatic rooms.
motive: No suspect motive.
secrets: Held back her strongest timing opinion until scene photographs were rechecked.
Believes the office wound is secondary.
statements: Primary internal injury does not fit the bronze model theory.
A lucid interval after head trauma is medically possible.
knownTimeline: 10:40 PM examined the body in place.
Next morning reviewed medical imaging.
Later connected Simon's speech errors to an earlier injury.

name: Lucas Bern
bio: A nervous volunteer used as extra hands for exhibit and crowd-flow tasks.
motive: No known motive.
secrets: Moved the Memory Arch into the corridor under instruction.
Was too embarrassed to admit he did not understand the brake system.
statements: Says Clara told him to move the Memory Arch into Service Corridor C at 6:30 PM.
Says the arch looked normal when he left it.
knownTimeline: 6:30 PM moved the Memory Arch into Service Corridor C.
7:00 PM returned to volunteer check-in.
7:42 PM distributing programs in the atrium.

name: Nadia Clarke
bio: A junior donor-relations associate who handled sponsor seating and message routing.
motive: Fear of blame for a false donor message.
secrets: Shared a message template with Clara earlier in the week.
Saw a donor message arrive from a staff-side device.
statements: Says the false donor message used an internal shorthand, not Ezra Cole's style.
Confirms Ezra's team had already left.
knownTimeline: 7:20 PM closed donor check-in.
7:39 PM saw a suspicious donor message enter the queue.
8:00 PM reset sponsor place cards.
